overview: These birds are identified as White-tailed Kites (Elanus leucurus). The scene likely depicts a food transfer between a male and female kite, a common courtship behavior. White-tailed Kites are small raptors known for hovering over open grasslands while hunting for rodents. They are recognized by their pale gray bodies, black shoulder patches, and red eyes. National Audubon Society +5 6 sites White-tailed Kite | Audubon Field Guide
